title: parahebe derwentiana subsp. maideniana
intro: Parahebe derwentiana subsp. maideniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, commonly known as a type of hebe or a flowering herb. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing sore throats. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of the plant are prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to aid in the treatment of various ailments.
